summarization.a: The chart illustrates the change in energy (ΔE) in kcal/mol for different steps in the reaction of chlorine groups at the surface with water, for the first (A), second (B), and third (C) chlorine group at the surface.

data_extraction.a:
| Reaction Step | ΔE (kcal/mol) A |ΔE (kcal/mol) B |ΔE (kcal/mol) C |
|---|---|---|---|
| Start | 0 |0 |0 |
| Complex | -18|-17|-15|
| TS | 4|4|0|
| HCl (a) | -1|-3|-2|
| HCl (g)| 0.5|5|7|

a.question: Which chlorine group is the easiest to remove and why?
a.answer: a	The third chlorine group, this can be seen by the fact that this reaction has the lowest energy barrier.

a.question: Are reaction products easily removed from the substrate?
a.answer: a	This depends on which chlorine group has been removed. For the last chlorine group, more energy is required to have a transision of HCl (a) to HCl (g).

a.question: What is the energy barrier for the removal of the second chlorine group?
a.answer: a	The energy barrier is 21 kcal/mol.

a.question: Can it be said that the physisorption of water is enhanced by hydroxyl groups at the surface?
a.answer: a	Yes this can be said, as the energy envolved decreases, for increasing number of removed chlorine groups.
